todaysEvents.summary: US-Iran MOU signing confirmed for June 19 in Switzerland; Trump lifts naval blockade effective June 14; Hormuz to reopen as 'permanently toll free' (NPR/CNN Jun 14-17)
todaysEvents.delta: Down: MOU text finalised June 12 via Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif. Trump announced deal June 14 (Sunday): US naval blockade lifted, Hormuz to reopen after signing ceremony June 19 in Switzerland. Deal structure: 60-day ceasefire extension + 60-day nuclear talks (Phase 2) + Iran oil sales resume + US sanctions waivers. Trump: Strait will be 'permanently toll free'. Escalation level lowered to 3 (Heightened) — active strikes ceased, diplomatic pathway restored. Signing is 2 days away.

todaysEvents.summary: Brent at 3-month lows $79.45 (+0.6% minor bounce); WTI $75.47; TTF €41.12 (-1.6%); Brent -19% from Jun 3 $98.57 peak (TradingEconomics Jun 17)
todaysEvents.delta: Down: Brent $79.45 (TradingEconomics Jun 17, +0.6% minor bounce after 4 consecutive down sessions). WTI ~$75.47 (fxdailyreport.com Jun 17). TTF €41.12 (TradingEconomics, -1.6%). Oil at three-month lows on deal signing expectations; market pricing Hormuz normalisation. Brent total decline: -19.4% from Jun 3 $98.57 peak in 14 days. iTraxx Asia IG ~142bp (est. -3bp); ASEAN HY ~495bp (est. -8bp). EM FX broadly stronger vs USD on risk-on. ASEAN Banks outperforming; energy equities underperforming.

todaysEvents.summary: Iran 'differing versions' of MOU text; nuclear sequencing unresolved; deputy FM: nuclear talks only after US releases frozen funds (Fortune/CNN Jun 14)
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Residual dispute risk. Iran's deputy FM said 60-day nuclear negotiations will begin only once the US releases billions in frozen funds — creating sequencing tension. Iran's Fars News agency disputed aspects of deal framing. Nuclear programme deferred to Phase 2 with no agreed mechanism. Physical mine-clearing ~6 months from signing; Ras Laffan LNG restart not before late Aug 2026. Deal fragile but Pakistan mediation track remains intact. Sign-or-collapse binary as of Jun 19.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: MOU signed June 19 in Switzerland (confirmed ceremony). US naval blockade fully lifted; Hormuz mine-clearing begins (~6 months). Iran oil sales resume on sanctions waivers. 60-day nuclear talks proceed in parallel. Brent normalises toward $70-80/bbl on supply resumption; P&I clubs re-enter Hormuz progressively; Ras Laffan restart late Aug 2026. Deal fragility risk: Iran 'differing versions' of text + nuclear sequencing dispute could delay implementation but not collapse the MOU. Probability raised to 50% on Jun 14 Trump announcement + Jun 19 signing confirmed.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Deal signed June 19 but implementation delays. Nuclear sequencing dispute causes Iran to withhold mine-clearing cooperation; Hormuz physically closed another 60-90 days past signing. Ras Laffan restart slips to Q4 2026. Brent stays $80-95/bbl as market discounts partial supply resumption. iTraxx Asia IG settles 130-155bp. JKM stays elevated at $15-18/MMBtu due to LNG supply lag. Frozen-assets sequencing dispute + Iran internal hardliner pushback are the main stress vectors. Probability 35% - deal pathway exists but implementation uncertain.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Deal collapses before or after Jun 19 signing. Iran hardliners or IRGC reject MOU; nuclear sequencing terms unworkable; frozen-assets release blocked in US Congress; Israel unilateral strikes restart hostilities. Hormuz remains closed; Brent spikes $110-130/bbl; mine-clearing timeline extends 12+ months. Probability lowered to 15% from 42% (D93) on deal announcement. Key tail triggers: Iran's differing MOU text causes signing collapse; congressional opposition to sanctions relief; IDF unilateral action restarts conflict.
